what_it_does: Defines a voice and style DNA record: invariant verbal tokens, casing and punctuation rules, tonal registers, operator-addressing voice, verbal forms, lexicon constraints, rules, invariants, and christening variants for generated or evaluated text.
observations: The file presents voice as a verbal medium bound to visual molecules, slots, chrome controls, and sequence roles.
It separates invariant voice_tokens from adjustable registers and from operator_voice address.
Registers are named immersive, tension, explanatory, credible, and precise, each with tonal description, word guidance, and evidence.
Forms define shapes, length budgets, casing, binding targets, examples, and evidence for titles, headings, leads, bullets, stats, captions, buttons, descriptions, and corpus-derived patterns.
Operator voice shifts person to second-person while preserving the invariant voice core, and includes field-level registers for decision-card-like slots.
Lexicon rules list preferred qualities, banned words, and a restrained superlative policy.
Invariants mix predicate-style checks with model-judged checks for hedging, length, punctuation, coinage, active voice, and adjective use near figures.
standouts: Voice is modeled as compositional data rather than only prose guidance.
The file explicitly treats verbal forms as bound to visual or interaction contexts.
Operator address is represented as a separate axis that composes with tonal register.
Evidence is carried inline throughout many records.
conventions: Uses short declarative descriptions and rule-like strings rather than executable schema definitions.
Uses underscore-prefixed note fields for interpretive context inside objects.
Keeps many examples neutral while preserving source evidence labels separately.
Defines display-text casing and punctuation as named conventions referenced by forms.
Uses arrays for word and sentence ranges.
Represents checker behavior as either predicate or model.
concerns: Several fields contain dense multi-clause prose, which may make programmatic parsing harder from this file alone.
Evidence references are embedded as free text rather than normalized records.
Some predicates are natural-language descriptions rather than directly testable expressions.
The operator_voice section includes calibration and external-context references whose status cannot be resolved from this file alone.
notes: This file is the verbal-side DNA ledger for how the system speaks, checks text, and binds copy forms to design contexts.
questions: What exact enum or schema owns the register names referenced here?
How are free-text evidence references resolved or validated?
How should operator_voice calibration status change once calibration is complete?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it defines voice as the verbal face of the design DNA and records how verbal forms, registers, and invariants compose with design contexts.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make verbal style governable as structured design DNA, linking copy shape, tone, evidence, and validation rules to visual and interaction substrates.
novelty: The distinct feature is the explicit coupling of voice forms to visual/interface substrates and the separation of brand register from operator-addressing person.
contribution: This file contributes the verbal substrate: invariant voice tokens, register names, copy-form shapes, operator-addressing rules, lexical constraints, and text-validation invariants that a fused system would keep as voice-facing records.
summary_for_embedding: This is a structured voice/style DNA JSON file defining invariant verbal tokens, registers, operator-addressing voice, copy forms, lexicon bans, rules, invariants, and evidence references for design-system text.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to turn deck-derived and interface-derived voice guidance into composable records that bind language to visual slots, molecules, sequence roles, and controls.
